name: reference-range-band
title: Reference Range Band
description: The normal range behind a series, plus labelled critical thresholds. Bounds and their population are supplied by the caller — never assumed.

props.name: axis
props.description: Which axis the bounds are read against.

props.name: low / high
props.description: Bounds of the normal range. Omit one for a one-sided range.

props.name: criticalBelow / criticalAbove
props.description: Drawn as labelled dashed lines that extend the axis domain, so a threshold never falls silently outside the visible range.

props.name: label
props.description: Names the band and its population — "Normal (adult, seated)". An unattributed band is an assertion the reader cannot check.

props.name: showBounds
props.description: Draws lines on the band edges.

props.name: fill
props.description: Low-chroma by default so the band never competes with the series.

clinicalNotes: A reference range is a property of the assay, the patient's age, and often their sex — it is never a constant, so every bound is passed in and `label` records which population it describes. Critical thresholds are drawn as labelled dashed lines rather than a red wash: colour alone is not a warning, and a colour-blind reader or a printed chart must carry the same information. Pair the band with a series whose out-of-range points differ in shape as well as colour (the demo does this). The band describes where values are expected to fall; it does not assert that a value inside it is clinically fine.
notes: Drop it inside any Recharts cartesian chart as a child — recharts 3 renders it directly. Composes chart.
